---
title: "SARIMA Model — Illinois Suicide Deaths"
output: html_notebook
---

## Setup
```{r setup}
packages <- c("tidyverse", "forecast", "zoo", "lubridate", "ggplot2", "readxl")

installed <- packages %in% rownames(installed.packages())
if (any(!installed)) install.packages(packages[!installed])
lapply(packages, library, character.only = TRUE)
```

## Load & Prep Data
```{r}
df_raw <- read_excel("~/Downloads/Illinois Suicide Deaths 1999-2026.xlsx")

df <- df_raw %>%
  rename(date_raw = date, deaths = deaths) %>%
  mutate(
    deaths = as.numeric(deaths),
    date   = as.Date(as.yearmon(date_raw, "%Y/%m"))
  ) %>%
  dplyr::select(date, deaths) %>%
  arrange(date) %>%
  filter(!is.na(deaths), !is.na(date))

cat("Date range:", format(min(df$date)), "to", format(max(df$date)), "\n")
cat("Total months:", nrow(df), "\n")
```

## Train / Test Split
```{r}
# Train: 1999/01 – 2023/12 (full years only)
# Test:  2024/01 – 2024/12 (full year 2024) #UPDATE: doesnt include provisional data

df_train <- df %>% filter(date <= as.Date("2023-12-01"))
df_test  <- df %>% filter(date >= as.Date("2024-01-01") & date <= as.Date("2024-12-01"))

cat("Training months:", nrow(df_train), "\n")
cat("Test months:    ", nrow(df_test),  "\n")

ts_train <- ts(df_train$deaths,
               start     = c(1999, 1),
               frequency = 12)

ts_test  <- ts(df_test$deaths,
               start     = c(2024, 1),
               frequency = 12)
```

## Fit SARIMA Model
```{r}
# auto.arima searches over ARIMA(p,d,q)(P,D,Q)[12] to minimize AICc
fit_sarima <- auto.arima(ts_train,
                         seasonal      = TRUE,
                         stepwise      = FALSE,
                         approximation = FALSE, 
                         trace         = TRUE,
                         D=1)

cat("\n=== SARIMA MODEL SUMMARY ===\n")
print(summary(fit_sarima))
```

## AIC / BIC
```{r}
cat("=== MODEL SELECTION CRITERIA ===\n")
cat("AIC:  ", round(AIC(fit_sarima), 2), "\n")
cat("BIC:  ", round(BIC(fit_sarima), 2), "\n")
cat("AICc: ", round(fit_sarima$aicc, 2), "\n")
```

## Forecast on Test Period
```{r}
h <- nrow(df_test)  # 12 months ahead

fc <- forecast(fit_sarima, h = h, level = c(80, 95))

# Build tidy forecast dataframe
df_fc <- tibble(
  date     = df_test$date,
  actual   = df_test$deaths,
  forecast = as.numeric(fc$mean),
  lo_80    = as.numeric(fc$lower[, "80%"]),
  hi_80    = as.numeric(fc$upper[, "80%"]),
  lo_95    = as.numeric(fc$lower[, "95%"]),
  hi_95    = as.numeric(fc$upper[, "95%"])
)

print(df_fc %>% mutate(across(where(is.numeric), round, 1)))
```

## Accuracy Metrics: RMSE & MAE
```{r}
cat("=== FORECAST ACCURACY (Test Set: 2024) ===\n") 

mae  <- mean(abs(df_fc$actual - df_fc$forecast))
rmse <- sqrt(mean((df_fc$actual - df_fc$forecast)^2))
mape <- mean(abs((df_fc$actual - df_fc$forecast) / df_fc$actual)) * 100

cat("MAE:  ", round(mae,  2), "deaths/month\n")
cat("RMSE: ", round(rmse, 2), "deaths/month\n")
cat("MAPE: ", round(mape, 2), "%\n")

# Also print forecast package accuracy table
accuracy(fc, ts_test)
```

## Ljung-Box Test (Residual Autocorrelation)
```{r}
cat("=== LJUNG-BOX TEST ===\n")
lb <- Box.test(residuals(fit_sarima), lag = 12, type = "Ljung-Box")
print(lb)

if (lb$p.value > 0.05) {
  cat("p =", round(lb$p.value, 4), "→ Residuals are white noise. Model is well-specified.\n")
} else {
  cat("p =", round(lb$p.value, 4), "→ Residuals show remaining autocorrelation. Consider refining model.\n")
}
```

## Residual Plots
```{r}
# Full residual diagnostic panel (ACF, histogram, time plot)
checkresiduals(fit_sarima)
```
```{r}
# Manual residual plots for more control
resid_df <- tibble(
  date     = df_train$date,
  residual = as.numeric(residuals(fit_sarima))
)

# Residuals over time
ggplot(resid_df, aes(x = date, y = residual)) +
  geom_line(color = "#2C7BB6", linewidth = 0.7) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed", color = "red") +
  labs(title = "SARIMA Residuals Over Time",
       subtitle = "Should appear as random noise around zero",
       x = NULL, y = "Residual") +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 13)

# Residual histogram
ggplot(resid_df, aes(x = residual)) +
  geom_histogram(aes(y = after_stat(density)), bins = 30,
                 fill = "#4575B4", alpha = 0.8, color = "white") +
  stat_function(fun = dnorm,
                args = list(mean = mean(resid_df$residual),
                            sd   = sd(resid_df$residual)),
                color = "#D7191C", linewidth = 1.1) +
  labs(title = "Residual Distribution",
       subtitle = "Red curve = normal distribution fit",
       x = "Residual", y = "Density") +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 13)
```

## Forecast vs Actual Plot
```{r}
# Historical + forecast + actual test values
ggplot() +
  # Training history (last 5 years for readability)
  geom_line(data = df_train %>% filter(date >= as.Date("2020-01-01")),
            aes(x = date, y = deaths),
            color = "#2C7BB6", linewidth = 0.8) +
  # 95% prediction interval
  geom_ribbon(data = df_fc,
              aes(x = date, ymin = lo_95, ymax = hi_95),
              fill = "#FDAE61", alpha = 0.3) +
  # 80% prediction interval
  geom_ribbon(data = df_fc,
              aes(x = date, ymin = lo_80, ymax = hi_80),
              fill = "#F46D43", alpha = 0.4) +
  # Forecast line
  geom_line(data = df_fc,
            aes(x = date, y = forecast),
            color = "#D7191C", linewidth = 1.1, linetype = "dashed") +
  # Actual test points
  geom_point(data = df_fc,
             aes(x = date, y = actual),
             color = "black", size = 2.5) +
  geom_line(data = df_fc,
            aes(x = date, y = actual),
            color = "black", linewidth = 0.9) +
  labs(title = "SARIMA Forecast vs Actual (2024)",
      subtitle = "Black = actual | Dashed red = forecast | Shading = 80% and 95% prediction intervals",
      x = NULL, y = "Deaths per Month") +
  scale_x_date(date_breaks = "6 months", date_labels = "%b %Y") +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 13)
```
